figures.caption: Figure 3: Comprehensive dataset static analysis: (a) Video duration distribution of both training and test sets, (b) segment duration distribution of both training and test sets, (c) pie chart of inconsistency events per video, (d) counts of 8 audio-visual inconsistency categories, and (e) examples of 8 audio-visual inconsistency categories.
Figure 4: TheAVID-Qwen architecture for omni-modal audiovisual inconsistency detection and reasoning.
Table 7: Training hyper-parameters for AVID-Qwen twostage fine-tuning.
Figure 8: Prompt for strategy planning agent.
Table 6: Class-conditional mapping between segment classes and applicable inconsistency categories.

Table 3: Comparison with existing models on the Segment and Full-Video tasks of the AVID benchmark. Detect: identify audio-visual inconsistency. Cla.: classify inconsistency category. R@𝛼 : Recall@1 at IoU threshold 𝛼 . mIoU: mean Intersection over Union. B/R/M: BLEU-4, ROUGE-L, and METEOR. 𝑆𝑀 : SODA-m score reflecting holistic dense understanding. All metrics are reported in percentage scale (values ×100).

Table 1: Comparison with representative audio-visual benchmarks from the perspective of inconsistency understanding. Video Form: segment-level clips or full-video samples. A/V Inconsistency: whether the benchmark explicitly targets audio-visual inconsistency. #Inc. Cat.: number of inconsistency categories. Event Grounding: whether event-level temporal localization annotations are provided. Inc. Reasoning: whether judging inconsistency requires explicit reasoning. G: generated. M: manual. V: visual. A: audio. S: speech. Caption modality indicates which modality the caption is based on. “–” denotes not applicable for benchmarks that do not target audio-visual inconsistency.
Figure 9: Prompt for multi-turn audio-visual inconsistency judgment.
Figure 7: Prompt for multi-turn audio-visual inconsistency detection in full videos.
Figure 5: Confusion matrices on segment-level 8-category inconsistency classification: (top row) closed-source models and ours, (bottom row) open-source models.

Figure 2: Overview of the AVID construction pipeline, consisting of three stages: (1) data preprocessing with temporal segmentation and class-aware labeling, (2) agentic generation with strategy planning and five specialized inconsistency injectors, and (3) human-in-the-loop alignment for quality control.
Table 8: Detailed test-time configuration for all evaluated large omni-models.
